content.explanation:
**Tenses Part 4 — Perfect Continuous Tenses:**

ये tenses बताते हैं कि कोई action **कब से** चल रही है और **अभी भी जारी है** (या तब भी थी)।

**1. Present Perfect Continuous:**
🇮🇳 मैं 2 घंटे से पढ़ रहा हूँ।
🇬🇧 I **have been studying** for 2 hours.
Formula: Subject + have/has been + V+ing

**2. Past Perfect Continuous:**
🇮🇳 वह 3 घंटे से काम कर रहा था जब boss आया।
🇬🇧 He **had been working** for 3 hours when the boss arrived.
Formula: Subject + had been + V+ing

**3. Future Perfect Continuous:**
🇮🇳 अगले साल तक मैं 5 साल से यहाँ काम कर रहा होऊँगा।
🇬🇧 By next year, I **will have been working** here for 5 years.
Formula: Subject + will have been + V+ing

**Key Signal Words:** for, since, how long, all day, all morning
rules: Present Perfect Continuous: have/has been + V+ing (duration up to now)
Past Perfect Continuous: had been + V+ing (duration before another past event)
Future Perfect Continuous: will have been + V+ing (duration by a future time)
Use "for" with duration (for 2 hours, for 3 years)
Use "since" with starting point (since morning, since 2020)
Perfect continuous = emphasis on DURATION of an ongoing action
Stative verbs cannot be used in continuous forms
content.memoryTrick:
**'For' = Duration, 'Since' = Starting Point**
'For 2 hours' = kitna time | 'Since morning' = kab se
Perfect Continuous = Have/Has/Had/Will Have + been + V+ing

examples.hindi: वह सुबह से बारिश हो रही है।
examples.english: It has been raining since morning.

examples.hindi: मैं कितने समय से यहाँ wait कर रहा हूँ?
examples.english: How long have I been waiting here?

examples.hindi: जब मैं पहुँचा, वे 2 घंटे से बात कर रहे थे।
examples.english: When I arrived, they had been talking for 2 hours.

examples.hindi: 2030 तक वह 10 साल से teach कर रही होगी।
examples.english: By 2030, she will have been teaching for 10 years.

examples.hindi: वह रात भर पढ़ रहा था।
examples.english: He had been studying all night.

examples.hindi: हम 3 बजे से meeting में हैं।
examples.english: We have been in the meeting since 3 o'clock.

mistakes.correct: I have been studying for 2 hours.
mistakes.why: Duration needs 'for', and it needs Present Perfect Continuous
Perfect continuous = had been + V+ing (not V2)

speakingTips: अपनी ongoing activities describe करो: 'I have been learning English for 35 days now!'
Stories में: 'I had been waiting for an hour when she finally called.'
Future goals: 'By 2026, I will have been working here for 3 years.'

vocabulary.example: I have been working for a long duration.
He had been working throughout the night.
She has been persistent in her efforts.
They had been enduring hardships for years.

practice.hindi: मैं रोज़ सुबह चाय पीता हूँ। (आदत)
practice.english: I drink tea every morning. (habit)
alternatives: i have tea every morning.

practice.hindi: वह अभी किताब पढ़ रही है।
practice.english: She is reading a book right now.
alternatives: she is studying a book now.

practice.hindi: मैं 5 साल से यहाँ रह रहा हूँ।
practice.english: I have been living here for 5 years.
alternatives: i have been staying here for 5 years.

practice.hindi: उसने कल एक नई कार खरीदी।
practice.english: She bought a new car yesterday.
alternatives: she purchased a new car yesterday.

practice.hindi: वह काम कर रहा था जब मैं पहुँचा।
practice.english: He was working when I arrived.
alternatives: he was doing work when i reached.

practice.hindi: ट्रेन जाने से पहले मैं स्टेशन पहुँच गया था।
practice.english: I had reached the station before the train left.
alternatives: i had arrived before the train departed.

practice.hindi: हम दो घंटे से इंतज़ार कर रहे थे।
practice.english: We had been waiting for two hours.
alternatives: we were waiting for two hours.

practice.hindi: मैं कल दिल्ली जाऊँगा।
practice.english: I will go to Delhi tomorrow.
alternatives: i am going to delhi tomorrow.

practice.hindi: कल इस वक्त वह सो रही होगी।
practice.english: She will be sleeping at this time tomorrow.
alternatives: she will be asleep this time tomorrow.

practice.hindi: शाम तक काम खत्म हो जाएगा।
practice.english: The work will have been finished by evening.
alternatives: the work will be done by evening.
practice.hint: Future Perfect = will have + past participle

practice.hindi: मैं तीन घंटे से पढ़ रहा हूँ।
practice.english: I have been studying for three hours.
alternatives: i have been reading for three hours.

practice.hindi: वह सुबह से कुछ नहीं खाया।
practice.english: She has not eaten anything since morning.
alternatives: she hasn't eaten since morning.

practice.hindi: हम अगले महीने तक प्रोजेक्ट पूरा कर चुके होंगे।
practice.english: We will have completed the project by next month.
alternatives: we will have finished the project next month.
practice.hint: Future Perfect = will have + past participle

practice.hindi: मुझे नहीं पता था कि वह वहाँ था।
practice.english: I did not know that he was there.
alternatives: i didn't know he was there.

practice.hindi: जब से मैं यहाँ आया हूँ, बहुत कुछ सीखा है।
practice.english: I have learned a lot since I came here.
alternatives: i have learnt so much since i arrived.

practice.hindi: वह कल से बीमार है।
practice.english: He has been sick since yesterday.
alternatives: he has been unwell since yesterday.

practice.hindi: मैंने कभी झूठ नहीं बोला।
practice.english: I have never told a lie.
alternatives: i have never lied.

practice.hindi: तुम जब तक वापस आओगे, मैं खाना बना चुका होऊँगा।
practice.english: By the time you return, I will have cooked food.
alternatives: i will have prepared food by the time you come back.
practice.hint: will have + cooked + by the time

practice.hindi: वह 2018 से इस कंपनी में काम कर रही है।
practice.english: She has been working in this company since 2018.
alternatives: she has been employed here since 2018.

practice.hindi: जब बारिश होगी, हम घर के अंदर होंगे।
practice.english: When it rains, we will be inside the house.
alternatives: when it rains, we will stay indoors.
practice.hint: When + Simple Present + will + base verb

practice.hindi: वह पिछले 3 सालों से डाइट पर है।
practice.english: She has been on a diet for the past 3 years.
alternatives: she has been dieting for 3 years.

practice.hindi: बच्चे घंटों से खेल रहे हैं।
practice.english: The children have been playing for hours.
alternatives: the kids have been playing for many hours.

practice.hindi: क्या तुम कभी कश्मीर गए हो?
practice.english: Have you ever been to Kashmir?
alternatives: have you ever visited kashmir?

practice.hindi: कल सुबह 8 बजे मैं जिम में होऊँगा।
practice.english: I will be at the gym at 8 AM tomorrow morning.
alternatives: i will be working out at 8 tomorrow.

practice.hindi: उसने कभी नहीं सोचा था कि यह होगा।
practice.english: He had never thought that this would happen.
alternatives: he had never imagined this would occur.

practice.hindi: वह दो घंटे से बात कर रही है।
practice.english: She has been talking for two hours.
alternatives: she has been speaking for 2 hours.

practice.hindi: मुझे नहीं पता कि वह कब आएगा।
practice.english: I do not know when he will come.
alternatives: i don't know when he will arrive.
practice.hint: do not know + when + will + base verb

practice.hindi: हम पाँच साल से एक साथ काम कर रहे हैं।
practice.english: We have been working together for five years.
alternatives: we have been teammates for 5 years.

practice.hindi: अगर तुम कल आओगे, हम मिलकर खाना खाएँगे।
practice.english: If you come tomorrow, we will eat together.
alternatives: if you visit tomorrow, we will have food together.
practice.hint: If + Simple Present + will + base verb

practice.hindi: जब मैं छोटा था, मैं पतंग उड़ाता था।
practice.english: When I was young, I used to fly kites.
alternatives: when i was a child, i would fly kites.
